content.problemStatement:
Chef is serving dishes at a feast full of colorblind people. He is adding dishes one by one to a long table to form a sequence of dishes; for each dish he adds, we know its color and deliciousness. A person with colorblindness ccc can only see dishes with colors in the range [c−K,c+K][c-K, c+K][c−K,c+K].

You should process QQQ queries. There are three types of queries:


1  c  d1\;c\;d1cd: Chef adds a dish with color ccc and deliciousness ddd to the front of the sequence.
2  c  d2\;c\;d2cd: Chef adds a dish with color ccc and deliciousness ddd to the back of the sequence.
3  c3\;c3c: Consider a person with colorblindness ccc. This person must choose an arbitrary contiguous subsequence of dishes on the table (possibly empty) and then eat all the dishes which this person can see. Chef wants to know the maximum possible total (summed up) deliciousness of the eaten dishes.
Note that all the people attending this feast are polite, so no dishes are actually eaten until Chef receives the answers to all of his queries.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two space-separated integers Q and K. The following Q lines describe queries. Each query starts with two space-separated integers b and C, where b denotes the type of query. If b = 1 or b = 2, they are followed by a space and an integer d. The value of c for this query is computed as c = C ⊕ ans, where ans is the answer to the previous query of type 3 or 0 if there have not been any queries of type 3 so far.
content.outputFormat: For each query of type 3, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum total deliciousness.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The maximum deliciousness is 6, which is the sum of the deliciousness of the dishes with colors 4 and 5.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a segment tree to store the maximum deliciousness for each color range. We will maintain two segment trees: one for the maximum deliciousness and one for the minimum deliciousness. When a new dish is added, we update the segment trees accordingly. When a query of type 3 is made, we use the segment trees to find the maximum deliciousness for the given color range.
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q * log(Q)) — each query takes O(log(Q)) time to update the segment trees and find the maximum deliciousness
solution.spaceComplexity: O(Q) — we need to store the segment trees, which have a size of O(Q)
